sentence_reviews.text: Learned vectors place semantically or functionally related entities near one another in a continuous space.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: PML book1 long TOC: word embeddings, latent semantic indexing, word2vec, GloVe, word analogies, and contextual word embeddings (lines 639-646); AIMA contents.html: word representations, word embeddings, and pretrained contextual representations (lines 628-657).
sentence_reviews.notes: The sources directly support vector word representations/embeddings; semantic/functional relatedness and continuous-space neighborhood wording are synthesized from the embedding context.

sentence_reviews.text: The role of Embeddings & distributed representations is to connect Linear algebra, Information theory to downstream capabilities including Positional, temporal & coordinate encodings, Sparse coding & compositional representations, Contrastive learning.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: PML book1 long TOC: linear algebra, information theory, word embeddings, sparse autoencoders, and self-supervised/contrastive-adjacent representation learning topics (lines 179-259, 622-646, and 585-587); AIMA contents.html: word representations/embeddings and pretrained contextual representations (lines 628-657).
sentence_reviews.notes: Linear algebra, information theory, word embeddings, and sparse autoencoders are supported. Positional/coordinate encodings, compositional representations, and contrastive learning are not directly supported by the inspected passages.

sentence_reviews.text: substrate for Positional, temporal & coordinate encodings
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: PML book1 long TOC: transformer positional encoding and sequence models (lines 486-490 and 463-478), plus linear algebra vector spaces (lines 206-259).
sentence_reviews.notes: Positional encoding and vector spaces are supported, but coordinate and temporal encodings as downstream embedding capabilities are not directly established.

sentence_reviews.text: substrate for Sparse coding & compositional representations
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: PML book1 long TOC: sparse autoencoders and sparse vector machines (lines 622-627 and 551-554), plus word embeddings (lines 639-646).
sentence_reviews.notes: Sparse representation topics are present, but sparse coding and compositional representations are not directly named or connected to embeddings in the inspected evidence.

sentence_reviews.evidence_location: PML book1 long TOC: self-supervised learning, unsupervised pretraining, matching networks, and word embeddings (lines 9-13, 585-587, 603-605, and 639-646).
sentence_reviews.notes: The inspected source supports embeddings and self-supervised learning, but does not directly mention contrastive learning.

sentence_reviews.text: Encoding choices can discard information, amplify bias, or make important invariances difficult to learn. Its direct dependencies include Linear algebra, Information theory.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: PML book1 long TOC: linear algebra, information theory, dimensionality reduction, word embeddings, and manifold learning (lines 179-259 and 607-646); AIMA contents.html: fairness/bias and word representations/embeddings (lines 745-749 and 628-657).
sentence_reviews.notes: Linear algebra, information theory, and embeddings are supported, and bias is a broad AI concern. Encoding information loss and invariance difficulty are not directly supported.

sentence_reviews.text: Embeddings & distributed representations rests on the formal or computational capabilities supplied by Linear algebra.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: PML book1 long TOC: Chapter 7 'Linear Algebra' and word embeddings (lines 206-259 and 639-646); AIMA contents.html: Appendix A.2 vectors/matrices/linear algebra and word representations/embeddings (lines 768-774 and 628-657).
sentence_reviews.notes: Embeddings as vectors and linear algebra background support the dependency; exact rationale wording is synthesized.

sentence_reviews.text: Embeddings & distributed representations rests on the formal or computational capabilities supplied by Information theory.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: PML book1 long TOC: information theory chapter, mutual information, sufficient statistics, and word embeddings (lines 179-205 and 639-646).
sentence_reviews.notes: Information theory is relevant to representation learning, but the inspected evidence does not directly establish it as a dependency for embeddings.

sentence_reviews.text: Embeddings & distributed representations consumes, emits, or organizes information using Scalars, vectors, matrices & tensors.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: PML book1 long TOC: linear algebra vector spaces/matrices and word embeddings (lines 206-259 and 639-646); AIMA contents.html: vectors/matrices/linear algebra and word representations/embeddings (lines 768-774 and 628-657).
sentence_reviews.notes: The vector/matrix nature of embeddings is well supported, though the full tensor phrasing is synthesized.

edge_reviews.notes: Embeddings are vector representations, and both sources provide linear-algebra background. Requires/core is defensible.
Information theory is relevant to representation learning, but the inspected evidence does not show it as a necessary prerequisite for embeddings. Common strength is plausible.
Embeddings are vector-valued representations, so the represents_with/common edge to tensors is supported.
